model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Revert the overgrown MLP back toward the stronger smaller configuration and increase batch size so each of the fixed 256 steps sees more CIFAR-10 examples.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch 128 was a strong gain, so the next low-risk move is to expose even more CIFAR-10 examples per fixed step by doubling the batch again.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Doubling the batch to 256 helped materially, so the next low-risk move is to push a bit further and expose more data per fixed optimization step.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size scaling has been the clearest win so far, and 512 should expose still more data per fixed optimizer step than 384.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The batch-size scaling trend is still positive, and 768 should give each fixed step more data without jumping all the way to the slowest extreme.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The batch-size trend is still positive, and 1024 further increases the amount of CIFAR-10 seen per fixed optimization step.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The large-batch regime is now likely bottlenecked by an underpowered step size, so a moderate LR increase is the cheapest next knob to try before changing the architecture or batch again.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher LR regressed, so this restores the best-known step size and removes the remaining weight decay to test whether the large-batch regime can fit a bit tighter.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With the large batch and short cosine-decayed run, the optimizer may benefit from a faster-adapting second moment estimate than beta2=0.999.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The faster-adapting Adam setting helped, so a slightly smaller step size may let the large-batch run settle a bit better near the end.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The lower LR regressed, so restore the better step size and try a slightly less aggressive beta2 than 0.95 to balance adaptation and smoothing in the large-batch regime.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.96)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The faster beta2=0.95 helped but 0.98 regressed, so a middle value may better balance responsiveness and smoothing in the large-batch regime.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1152","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.96)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The best Adam beta was 0.95, and batch scaling is still the main lever; this tries a slightly larger batch while restoring the more responsive beta2 setting.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1280","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1152","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The batch-size scaling trend is still the strongest signal, and 1280 should expose a bit more data per fixed step while keeping the rest of the working optimizer setup intact.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1408","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1280","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The batch-size scaling trend has been the most reliable improvement path, and a modest increase beyond 1280 is the lowest-risk final adjustment.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}
